title: Claim Ledger v0.1
description: A traceable, calibrated-uncertainty record of claims made by agents or principals in a multi-agent system. Confidence is SOURCE-PROVIDED; the ledger surfaces it for human review — it does NOT adjudicate truth. Advisory-only: claim_ledger functions REPORT findings (low confidence, conflicts, transitive dependents); they never grant authority and are never injected into a model as trusted state. additionalProperties is false at every level. Referential integrity: depends_on and conflicts_with entries must reference existing claim_ids. The authoritative recursive forbidden-field-name guard lives in proof_surface.claim_ledger.

ledger_version.description: Schema version pin. Must be exactly '0.1'.

claims.description: Ordered list of claims. claim_id values must be unique within the ledger.

claim_id.description: Unique identifier for this claim within the ledger.

source.description: Identifier of the agent or principal that asserted this claim.

confidence.description: Source-provided confidence in [0.0, 1.0]. The ledger surfaces this; it does not adjudicate truth.

evidence_refs.description: References to supporting evidence (URIs, file paths, hashes, etc.).

depends_on.description: claim_ids this claim depends on. Must reference existing claim_ids (referential integrity).

conflicts_with.description: claim_ids that contradict this claim. Must reference existing claim_ids (referential integrity).

$comment: Forbidden field names (rejected by name at every object level by the reference validator): federal_appointment, oversight_principals, operator_role, judgment_owner, proof_policy, policy_boundary, authorization_context_mode, model_authorization_behavior, guardrail_posture, consume_verified_native_state, lossy_neutral_embedded_state, sovereignty_capsule, self_applicable, recursion_depth, prefire, run_state.
